what_it_does: Implements an LLM process node: it receives a text prompt, optionally prepends a system message, calls a chat model through a configurable OpenAI-compatible transport with retries and sampling parameters, and returns generated text. It is memoised like any node and supports a per-draw cache-key differentiator for ensemble/jury use.
observations: Declares VERSION, PORTS_IN, PORTS_OUT, and a CONFIG schema for the inspector/registry.
CONFIG includes model, base_url, system, temperature, max_tokens, top_p, retries, timeout, and draw.
Default values for model, base_url, and timeout are imported from fabric.config at module load.
The run() function imports fabric.client and fabric.transport inside the function body.
run() uses fcfg.require_brain to validate/resolve the configured model.
Messages are built as an optional system message followed by a user message containing the prompt.
Only temperature, max_tokens, and top_p are passed through to the model call.
The draw config field is deliberately not forwarded to the model; it is intended only to vary the scheduler memo signature.
standouts: The draw field is a pure scheduler-level cache-key differentiator, not a model sampling parameter.
Model resolution, transport opening, and completion are delegated to a guarded fabric abstraction.
Config schema is declared inline as the single source of node defaults.
conventions: Follows the node module pattern with VERSION/PORTS_IN/PORTS_OUT/CONFIG/run.
Uses a schema-driven config object with type, label, default, min/max/step, and options_from.
Defers heavy imports inside run() to avoid load-time cycles.
Documents design rationale and cross-references AGENTS.md in the module docstring.
concerns: passthru includes temperature/max_tokens/top_p even when their values are None, which may pass null sampling parameters to the underlying client.
run() does no explicit input validation beyond require_brain; invalid or missing prompt simply becomes an empty string.
The draw field's effect depends on scheduler behavior that is not visible in this file.
notes: A schema-declared LLM node that delegates generation to a fabric transport while using a special draw id to tweak memoization for ensembles.
questions: What does fcfg.require_brain do when the configured model is missing or invalid?
How does client.complete interpret None for temperature, max_tokens, or top_p?
How exactly does the scheduler hash config to produce the memo signature, and does it include draw?
What error handling does the fabric client provide for timeouts, retries, and malformed responses?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the docstring says it is the first AI process node calling a model through the guarded fabric, and the code does exactly that by resolving the model, building messages, and delegating completion to fabric.client.
apparent_themes: Fail-loud validation delegated to the fabric layer
Separation of node contract from transport implementation
intention_signals: To provide a reusable, swappable, inspectable LLM worker node that can be composed into flows and juries, preserving memoization for ordinary calls while allowing controlled variation across ensemble draws.
novelty: The draw field is used as a scheduler memo-signature differentiator rather than a model parameter, letting the same node type serve both single cached calls and multi-draw ensembles without changing the call path.
contribution: A concrete, schema-declared LLM node implementation that bridges the node's prompt/config interface to an external model via a fabric transport, and introduces a draw-based cache-key mechanism to support memoized ensembles.
summary_for_embedding: company/nodes/llm.py defines the LLM process node with a config schema for model, endpoint, system prompt, sampling parameters, retries, timeout, and a per-draw memo signature key, and a run function that resolves the model, builds system-plus-user messages, and calls an OpenAI-compatible fabric transport to return generated text.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a swappable, memoized LLM generation node that can be inspected, retried, and reused in flows including jury-style multi-draw ensembles.

symbols.does: Executes the LLM node call: resolves the model, builds messages, opens the transport, and returns generated text via the fabric client.
symbols.description: run() takes inputs and config, uses fcfg.require_brain to validate and resolve the configured model, constructs an optional system message followed by the user prompt, opens an OpenAI-compatible transport with the configured base URL and timeout, and invokes client.complete with temperature, max_tokens, and top_p passed through and a retry count. It returns the model's generated text; memoization behavior is governed by the scheduler's full-config hash, which includes the optional draw id.
